name: West Pomeranian Voivodeship (Poland)

colors.symbolism: the central field bearing the Pomeranian griffin

description: Three vertical stripes — red, white, red — with the red griffin of West Pomerania charged on the central white stripe.
history: Adopted by the West Pomeranian Voivodeship after its formation in 1999, restoring the medieval heraldic device of the Duchy of Pomerania.

features.symbolism: red and white drawn from the historic colours of Pomerania and Poland
the historic emblem of the Duchy of Pomerania
